coreIssue: This growth conversation is about whether Toby can translate strong Design Lead execution into visible, org-level leadership through clearer decision ownership and cross-functional influence.
openingQuestion: What kind of decisions and cross-functional moments would you want to own more visibly to show the next level of design leadership?
listenFor: whether he names a specific decision he held or deferred in the last quarter
whether he points to a concrete stakeholder or forum where he stayed less visible than he wanted
if they describe cross-functional impact through a specific alignment moment, tradeoff, or avoided surprise rather than only shipped work
avoid: do not frame being passed over last cycle as the main explanation before Toby has described his view
do not leave with a broad development list that lacks one lead-level ownership area and visible cross-functional expectation
goodOutcome: You and Toby have agreed one lead-level growth contract: a concrete area of end-to-end decision ownership plus the cross-functional influence expected to make that scope visible beyond his immediate team.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Toby delivered strongly but did not shape an org-level decision, so you can ground the discussion in observable behaviour rather than general impressions.
